articles.title: WORKSHOP
articles.summary: As the software landscape evolves rapidly with AI, are you equipped with the skills to thrive in SOFTWARE 3.0? Attend INTERVIEW KICKSTART’S SOFTWARE 3.0: THE FUTURE OF SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT [4] on MARCH 10, 2024, 5:30 PM PST, by Ashish Kaila, FAANG+ leader, and SDE.

articles.title: REGISTER FOR THE WORKSHOP (LIMITED SPOTS, HURRY!)
INFLECTION 2.5
articles.summary: Inflection has launched a new model to power its personal assistant, Pi. The model performs within 94% of GPT-4 with impressive reasoning scores on benchmarks. Inflection claims it only took 40% of the compute to train relative to GPT-4. An interesting insight from this post is that the average conversation with Pi lasts 33 minutes.

articles.title: ANSWER AI’S NEW OPEN SOURCE FINE-TUNING TOOL
articles.summary: Answer AI has released a new FSDP/QLoRA training tool that makes it possible to train 70B parameter models on consumer GPUs. It has open sourced the code and made it easy to run locally or on runpod.

articles.title: COHERE X ACCENTURE
articles.summary: Cohere and Accenture are collaborating to bring cohere embedding technology to more than 9,000 enterprise clients.

articles.title: TINYBENCHMARKS: EVALUATING LLMS WITH FEWER EXAMPLES
articles.summary: Evaluating language models is typically done with hand-curated benchmarks. Some of these benchmarks are quite large, with some at over 14k examples, which leads to high expense and noise in evaluation. This work shows that you can reliably evaluate language model performance with as few as 100 examples from popular benchmarks.

articles.title: DEEP DIVE: TRAINING MODELS AT SCALE
articles.summary: A great tutorial on the different types of parallelism for training large models at scale.

articles.title: TEACHING ROBOTS TO IMITATE
articles.summary: DP3 introduces a cutting-edge approach to imitation learning, combining 3D visual data and diffusion policies to teach robots complex skills efficiently.

articles.title: SCALE AUTOMATED WEB TESTING WITHOUT SCALING HEADCOUNT
articles.summary: You don’t need an army of testers to build, run, and maintain more tests. QA Wolf (4.8 ⭐ on G2) [11] brings AI-level scale and efficiency to testing, with unlimited parallel test runs on their cloud infrastructure and a zero-flake guarantee. 80% automated test coverage [11] in just 4 months. Trusted by Cohere, Autotrader, Salesloft and many others.

articles.title: NOUS RESEARCH GENSTRUCT MODEL (HUGGINGFACE HUB)
articles.summary: Genstruct is a model that can be used to generate instructions from natural language text. This can then be used to train downstream models for specific useful tasks.

articles.title: FRUCTOSE
articles.summary: Fructose is a Python package for creating dependable strongly-typed interfaces around LLM calls.

articles.title: ENHANCING LLM PERFORMANCE THROUGH COLLABORATION
articles.summary: A novel method enables large language models to work together by taking turns generating text, token by token. This strategy allows models to leverage their unique strengths and expertise for various tasks, leading to improved performance across instruction-following, domain-specific questions, and reasoning challenges.

articles.title: BENCHMARKING MULTIHEADED ATTENTION IMPLEMENTATIONS (JUPYTER NOTEBOOK)
articles.summary: There is an almost 10x difference in speed between different implementations of the popular multi-headed attention module used in modern LLMs. This notebook shows a few and benchmarks their performance.

articles.title: AI IS LIKE WATER: NECESSARY, UBIQUITOUS, AND SO SIMILAR
articles.summary: Technology alone is no longer enough to provide a competitive advantage for GenAI companies. This makes GenAI and bottled water similar, as the underlying product is essentially the same. The key differentiators must come from factors such as user experience, distribution, perceived value to customers, branding, and marketing.

articles.title: SORA, GROQ, AND VIRTUAL REALITY
articles.summary: A few years ago, the concept of the metaverse seemed like it was just fiction from Ernest Cline's book, and Facebook's metaverse push seemed ill-fated. Today, things feel different. Sora generates complex video scenarios and Groq's deterministic chips reduce machine-learning algorithms for faster processing. Together, the convergence of these advancements pushes us closer to achieving real-time video simulation and true virtual reality.

articles.title: MICROSOFT INTRODUCES COPILOT AI CHATBOT FOR FINANCE WORKERS IN EXCEL AND OUTLOOK
articles.summary: Microsoft has unveiled a new Copilot chatbot tailored for finance tasks, with a public preview to come before pricing details are announced. The AI can conduct variance analysis, reconcile data in Excel, and expedite Outlook collections, integrating with SAP and Dynamics 365. The tool aims to reduce time spent on manual tasks for finance teams, potentially transforming financial planning and analysis efficiency.

articles.title: MICROSOFT’S MISTRAL DEAL BEEFS UP AZURE WITHOUT SPURNING OPENAI
articles.summary: Microsoft's €15 million investment in French AI firm Mistral while continuing its strong partnership with OpenAI illustrates its strategy to diversify and bolster Azure's AI offerings and gain a foothold in the European market ahead of the EU's AI Act. Azure aims to become a model garden, offering a variety of AI models to attract developers and compete with other cloud services. The Mistral deal also positions Microsoft strategically within European AI regulatory frameworks.

articles.title: US REGULATORS INVESTIGATE WHETHER OPENAI INVESTORS WERE MISLED, SAY REPORTS
articles.summary: The SEC is investigating OpenAI, focusing on CEO Sam Altman's internal communications after accusations that he misled investors. This inquiry coincides with global attention on OpenAI's ChatGPT and scrutinizes the competitive impact of AI investments by OpenAI and tech giants in the sector.
